opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I had this sudden, ripping pain on my right side down low, and I felt so dizzy I almost passed out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden severe right-sided pelvic pain with dizziness and presyncope.

symptom_narrative.character: sharp, severe, unilateral right lower abdominal/pelvic pain
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: scant dark vaginal spotting since yesterday, felt dizzy and nearly fainted walking to the bathroom, new pain in the tip of the right shoulder
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: assumed her period was just irregular, about 6 weeks since her last period
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: lying completely still on the bed, cannot stand without severe dizziness

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: took one dose with no relief

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: partner present and very concerned, sexually active

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened, hadn't considered she might be pregnant, worried something is seriously wrong

access_barriers.transport: partner present but patient cannot safely mobilise given dizziness
